title: Beam Therapeutics Q1 2026 Earnings - Loss Narrows with Strong Revenue
text: Beam reported a net loss of $94.3 million ($0.91 per share) in Q1 2026, narrower than the $108.3 million loss ($1.23 per share) in Q1 2025. The company maintained $1.2 billion in cash and marketable securities as of March 31, 2026. Operating expenses increased: R&D spending reached $104.5 million (vs. $98.8 million year-over-year), while G&A expenses climbed to $34.4 million (vs. $27.9 million). BEAM-302 (Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency) selected 60 mg as the optimal biological dose with pivotal cohort enrollment planned for H2 2026, targeting approximately 50 patients. Risto-cel (Sickle Cell Disease) Phase 1/2 BEACON trial data was published in the New England Journal of Medicine with BLA submission expected as early as year-end 2026. BEAM-304 (PKU) IND filing is anticipated in 2026 and BEAM-301 (GSDIa) initial clinical data is expected in 2026. The company expects its cash position, including $100 million already received and an anticipated additional $100 million from Sixth Street, will support operations into mid-2029.
